---
title: "spark"
---


-   Create a spark connection with `spark_connect()` and assign it to a variable usually "sc"
    -   Can either use master="local" or if you are in databricks you can use master="databricks"
-   Then you can import your data referencing your spark connection
    - for inline data you can simply reference copy_to(sc,df,df_name) and assign to a object (typically sdf prefix)
-   From there you can use all your standard dblyr type functions however sometimes certain functions won't work
-   The master trick here is to use the `sparklyr::spark_apply()` function
    -   pass the df object to as argument and then pass funciton or custom function
    -   I don't think there are room for additoinal parameters? but I may be wrong
-   
    


```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(sparklyr)


df <- readr::read_csv("/home/hagan/Downloads/pop23.csv",name_repair = janitor::make_clean_names) %>% 
     filter(
    sex!=0
    ,age<99
    )

sc <- sparklyr::spark_connect(master="local")

df_spk <- sparklyr::copy_to(sc,df,"df_spk",overwrite = TRUE) 



extract_year_make_longer  <- function(.data,.col) {

  .data %>% 
  select(
    age
    ,sex
    ,any_of(
      c({{.col}})
      )
    ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(-c(1:2))
}

df_spk %>%
  extract_year_make_longer(.,col_names) %>% 
  mutate(year=dplyr::%>%)

gsub(pattern = ,x = "hello123",replacement = "")

col_names <- c("popestimate2020","popestimate2021","popestimate2022")
df %>% 
  extract_year_make_longer("popestimate2021")


df_spk %>%sparklyr::spark_apply(
  function(df) 
    dplyr::mutate(tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::select(df,sex:age,popestimate2020),-c(sex:age)),year=readr::parse_number(name))
)




srk_fun <- function(e) {
      library(magrittr)
    
e %>% 
   dplyr::select(age:sex,popestimate2020) %>% 
   tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(1:2)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(year=readr::parse_number(name))
  }

 sparklyr::spark_apply(
  x=df_spk
  ,f=srk_fun
) %>% collect()

 df %>% srk_fun
 
rowwise_fun <- function(e){

library(magrittr)
 e %>% 
   dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
   dplyr::mutate(test=sum(dplyr::c_across(x:z)))
}

diamonds_spk <- sparklyr::copy_to(sc,diamonds,"diamonds_sprk")

 sparklyr::spark_apply(
  x=small_diamonds_sdf
  ,f=rowwise_fun
)

 small_diamonds_sdf <- diamonds_spk %>% head(100)
 diamonds %>% 
   rowwise_fun
 
 
 
diamonds
 
 
 


```
